Okay, so what is positioning? So positioning is basically your perceived area of position. I guess I'm trying to figure out a better way to say this, but what's essential with high ticket packages, high ticket programs, is positioning. So one of the things that happen, like people love referrals because they're actually already pre-positioned. That referral knows the value of that coach in their mind because they help their friend out and so they treat them with the reverence or the positioning or power that they should have because they've already have trust. But how do you do that with a cold market? How do you get a cold market to bend their knee and trust you and respect you enough to pay high ticket prices? So there's a process all around this that you must follow and that's why the sales process for this is very different than the sales process for low ticket stuff because there you're just going for volume and getting people in the door. But with this it's very particular about how we go about it, which is it's actually the exact same process that we took you through in order to buy this. And so that's what I'm basically teaching you, which is positioning because most people, well we do get a lot of referrals, but a lot of people, probably about half the people that come into our program, still didn't hear about us before in advertisement. And then within two phone calls are handing us five figures or more over the phone. And so that's a big deal if you think about that. Like I saw an ad, I got a one phone call and then I gave someone that I've never met before five figures. Like how does it happen? And so it happens with positioning. And so everything's very deliberate in terms of what the process you're taking the prospect through. And everything has to do with credibility and authority. And that's where the testimonials and having all these, all the social proof is so necessary because then you can reinforce that positioning, right? But the main step is having a two-step close, which we have an entire section dedicated to. But I just wanted to go over this just as an overarching concept because you're going to have to treat the entire process from the eyeball of positioning, just how is my prospect seeing me? How are they perceiving my business? And how are they perceiving me as an expert or someone who needs their business versus them needing me and my service? Okay, hopefully that makes sense. Okay, cool. I'll go to the next video.
